name: dumbbell single leg deadlift with stepbox support
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand.
Place your left foot on a stepbox or elevated surface behind you.
Keeping your back straight and core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, lowering the dumbbell towards the ground.
As you lower the dumbbell, simultaneously lift your left leg behind you, maintaining a straight line from head to heel.
Lower the dumbbell until you feel a stretch in your right hamstring, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand. Place your left foot on a stepbox or elevated surface behind you. Keeping your back straight and core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, lowering the dumbbell towards the ground. As you lower the dumbbell, simultaneously lift your left leg behind you, maintaining a straight line from head to heel. Lower the dumbbell until you feel a stretch in your right hamstring, then return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
